text:
Market today: Chip stocks are getting hit hard - Samsung and SK Hynix down 7%+ overnight, and that rout is spreading from Wall Street. This is the real driver today. Your semiconductor-heavy book is directly in the crosshairs.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is your biggest pain point - it's a 3x leveraged ETF decaying against you, and on margin that's a double amplification of hurt; stop is $200, watch it closely. SPAL (-13.72%) is brand-new, very volatile, and already near its tight $21.38 stop - one bad tick and it's gone. SMH (-2.5%) is underwater with the chip rout as direct headwind. All three need eyes on today.

Watch: Whether the Samsung/SK Hynix selloff bleeds into US chip names at open. Also watch SOXL's $200 stop - if it cracks, no heroics.

Bottom line: Chip rout is live and hitting your exact book - protect capital, let your stops do their job.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is your line - if price touches it today given the chip rout headline, respect it and exit. No adding into a 3x leveraged ETF bleeding on margin.
SPAL: Stop $21.38 is razor-thin from current price $21.70 - this leveraged ETF moves fast; be ready to exit immediately if triggered, no second-guessing on margin.
No new buys today - chip sector under active pressure; scanner setups like TSM and ARM exist but buying into a spreading rout without stabilization is forcing a trade.
